description: Comment lines - full-line decode-side comments stripped before structural interpretation

tests.name: strips comment line between tabular rows without terminating rows
tests.note: The comment is not counted as a row and does not end the tabular scope

tests.name: strips comment line between header and first row
strips comment line whose indentation is not a multiple of indentSize
tests.note: Comment lines are exempt from the §12 strict indentation check

tests.name: strips outdented comment inside nested object scope without closing it
tests.input:
user:
  id: 1
# outdented note
  name: Ada
tests.note: A depth-0 comment between depth-1 fields does not close the object

tests.name: strips comment inside list array without counting as item
strips comment before root array header
tests.note: Root-form discovery runs on the comment-stripped sequence

tests.name: strips comment containing an unterminated quote without unescaping
tests.note: Comment text is discarded without interpretation; the quote never reaches the tokenizer

tests.name: decodes document of only comments to empty object
decodes comments and blank lines only to empty object
decodes former root scalar starting with hash to empty object
tests.note: Wire break vs v3: the line is a comment, leaving an empty document

tests.name: throws when a stripped hash-leading row breaks the declared count
tests.note: The first cell makes the whole line a comment; only one row remains for [2]

tests.name: drops hash-leading row silently in non-strict mode
tests.note: Corruption path for v3 documents with unquoted hash-leading first cells; see MIGRATION.md

tests.name: throws on tab-indented hash line, which is not a comment
tests.note: Only spaces (U+0020) may precede the #; the tab makes this a regular line, and tabs in indentation error in strict mode (§12)

tests.name: parses quoted hash-leading first cell as data, not comment
parses hyphen list item with hash-leading token as string
tests.note: "#" is not the line's first non-space character; comments are full-line only

tests.name: parses hash after key-value colon as string value
tests.note: No inline comments; the unquoted token decodes as the string #x

tests.name: parses hash mid-value as data, not a trailing comment
round-trips quoted hash-leading values in field and inline array positions
